texto: I spoke a word in anger To one who was my friend, Like a knife it cut him deeply, A wound that was hard to mend. That word, so thoughtlessly uttered, I would we could both forget, But its echo lives and memory gives The recollection yet. How many hearts are broken, How many friends are lost By some unkind word spoken Before we count the cost! But a word or deed of kindness Will repay a hundredfold. For it echoes again in the hearts of men And carries a joy untold.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a C.A. Lufburrow: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I spoke a word in anger To one who was my friend, Like a knife it cut him deeply, A wound that wa…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, anger, hearts pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e amizade.
